title: I Ching reference
wikitext:
[]: I Ching reference?             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Dec 31, 2005 9:21 am A Â¼ way through. Some observations: During episode 23 (Rei III), we are given a glimpse into the Artificial Evolution Laboratory, where Rei was infused with Angel. In these scenes there are a few shots around the room, including this one. When I saw those three lines I thought "thatâ€™s a trigram." When I opened my copy of the Book of Changes to see which â€˜elementâ€™ this trigram represented it was water/the moon (water represents, it says in this book anyway, passion/danger). Seems to carry on Reiâ€™s whole water/moon/blood theme along well. This is from the I Ching, which also ties in with Terence McKennaâ€™s Novelty Theory and Incunabula (if it ever existed). ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- A few days back now I was fiddling with a printed copy of Natureâ€™s First Pattern, which you can download and print off from here and here, this is what appeared after a few minutes. 1: Ramiel (viewed from an odd angle, but it's still hir) 2: An AT Field 3: Seal of Soloman (Star of David) All encoded into this patternâ€¦ Makes sense that your soulâ€™s boarder/defence would be a reflection of Natureâ€™s underlying field pattern (simple building blocks). Make of it what you will. Edit: Information about the 5th trigram: A solid line in between two broken lines symbolize water. The two broken lines represent the depression of earth (river banks). The solid line in the middle represents motion. This creates the image of water flowing in a river. In addition, this kua also represents the moon. Source. Also, putting the terms: 'Chaos' or 'physics' with 'I Ching' into Google yields interesting results. I have one question: Does anyone have an idea of what this actually is?

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Sun Jan 01, 2006 11:28 pm Just out of curiousity, what program were you fiddling around with and how were you fiddling with it?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jan 01, 2006 11:56 pm I would have said that it just looks like some scraggly lines, but the source you supply that states that the most similar trigam represents both water and the moon is certainly enough to give anyone pause. Rei is consistently and recuurently linked with the moon throughout the series, and we are told in this very scene that "Light and Water , which make up Rei's deep mentality, reflect the image of this place." Could this really be an overt trigam reference? It is certainyl worth looking for more such references elsewhere in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Mon Jan 02, 2006 5:11 am I suppose you were expecting me to post here. I shall post anyway. I have considered that diagram's significance towards an A.T Field before. While it does seem apparant that there is a connection, especially the Seal of Solomon is also known as the Shield of David, there's actually a huge difference. All A.T Fields are OCTogonal. The diagram is HEXagonal. http://perso.wanadoo.fr/tom.series.evangelion/nge/at_field.jpg http://www.superhero-zone.com/ProductImages/Neon%20Genesis/NGE-ThirdAngelSachiel-AF.jpg Other than this, everything else seems connect the two quite strongly, like the SoS encircling the seed of life the same way an A.T Field envelops a soul.

[]: 64 Hexagrams formed from 8 Trigrams             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jan 06, 2006 4:26 pm The AT Fieldâ€™s spatial distortions are octagonal in most scenes, apart from this one. I tried to find an octagonal shape within NFP but nothing elegant appeared. BLACKANGEL32076 wrote: Just out of curiousity, what program were you fiddling around with and how were you fiddling with it?

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Mon Jan 09, 2006 12:32 pm it is interesting that Ramiel of all Angels should have a Hexagonal A.T Field.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jan 13, 2006 3:00 pm I have no idea why Ramiel would be the only (maybe) messenger to have this type of ATF (except for what I wrote in my first post). Also, as I posted a while back, the ATF could be a direct reference to Metatron's Cube. This is from wikipedia: Each of the thirteen circles (referred to as 'nodes') is connected to each other node with a single line, creating a total of seventy-eight lines. Within the cube, many other shapes can be found, including two dimensional models of the five platonic solids. In early kabbalist scriptures, Metatron supposedly forms the cube from his soul , which can later be seen in Christian art, where it appears on his chest or floating behind him. Metatron's cube is also considered a holy glyph, and was often drawn around an object or person to ward off demons and satanic powers . This idea is also present in alchemy, in which the cube was favoured as a containment circle or creation circle .
